confirmed.title: FileInput drop-zone instruction text hardcoded in English
confirmed.detail: Visible empty-state copy `Drag a file here or click to browse` is a raw English literal. The same component already imports/uses `t()` for its aria-label (line 41) and the browse/choose-another labels (lines 65-67), so the translator is in scope but this primary instruction string was missed. NOT in ledger (ledger covered FileInput lines 39 and 65 only). Visible text, end-user facing. Suggested: t('arqel.fields.file.drop_hint', 'Drag a file here or click to browse').
confirmed.why_real: Verified at /home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/packages-js/fields-js/src/file/FileInput.tsx:62. The drop-zone empty-state instruction `Drag a file here or click to browse` is a raw English literal, end-user-visible, while the same component already uses the in-scope translator t() for its aria-label (line 41) and the Browse/Choose-another button labels (lines 65-67). This is a genuine missing-i18n defect. Ledger check: FileInput appears only at line 39 (round 4, aria-label 'File upload') and line 65 (round 7, visible button text 'Choose another file'/'Browse'). Line 62's drop-zone instruction text is a distinct string not covered by R1-R7. The file is under packages-js/fields-js/, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*, so it is not a vendored shadcn primitive.

confirmed.title: CommandPalette footer keyboard-hint labels hardcoded in English
confirmed.detail: The palette footer renders three raw keyboard-hint spans: `↑↓ navigate` (line 356), `↵ select` (line 357), `esc close` (line 358). The verbs 'navigate', 'select', 'close' are visible English UI copy. CommandPalette already uses `t()` extensively (its sr-only heading/status at lines 274/285/291/297 were fixed in the ledger), so the translator is available; these footer hints were missed. NOT in ledger. Visible text. Suggested keys: t('arqel.palette.hint_navigate', '↑↓ navigate'), t('arqel.palette.hint_select', '↵ select'), t('arqel.palette.hint_close', 'esc close') — keep the glyph, translate only the word.
confirmed.why_real:
Verified at /home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/packages-js/ui/src/palette/CommandPalette.tsx:356-358. The footer renders three raw English keyboard hints: ↑↓ navigate, ↵ select, esc close. These are visible (not sr-only) user-facing UI strings inside a rendered
. The component already imports/uses t() throughout (lines 277, 287, 293, 301), so the translator is available and these were genuinely missed. Ledger (i18n-fixed-ledger.json) contains four CommandPalette.tsx entries — lines 274, 285, 291, 297 (sr-only h2, input placeholder, live-region, listbox aria-label) — none of which cover the footer hints at 356-358; novel. File path is packages-js/ui/src/palette/, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*, so it is not a vendored primitive. Reproduces, novel, user-facing, non-vendored.

confirmed.title: CommandPalette 'select' keyboard hint hardcoded
confirmed.detail: `↵ select` — visible English verb 'select' in the palette footer hint row, not wrapped in t(). Companion to the line-356 finding; reported separately per line. NOT in ledger. Suggested: t('arqel.palette.hint_select', '↵ select').
confirmed.why_real: Verified at /home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/packages-js/ui/src/palette/CommandPalette.tsx:357: `↵ select` is a visible English verb in the palette footer hint row, not wrapped in t(). The sibling spans (line 356 '↑↓ navigate', line 358 'esc close') are equally hardcoded, confirming the whole hint footer is un-i18n'd. This is non-vendored code (palette/, not shadcn/*), user-facing (footer is rendered, id=hintId referenced via aria-describedby on the dialog), and reproduces. Ledger scan: CommandPalette.tsx entries cover only lines 274 (sr-only h2), 285 (input placeholder), 291 (live region), 297 (listbox aria-label) across rounds 1-7. Neither line 356 nor 357 appears in the ledger, so it is novel. Confidence high.

confirmed.title: CommandPalette 'esc close' keyboard hint hardcoded
confirmed.detail: `esc close` — visible English 'esc close' in the palette footer hint row, not wrapped in t(). Companion to the line-356/357 findings. NOT in ledger. Suggested: t('arqel.palette.hint_close', 'esc close').
confirmed.why_real: Verified at /home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/packages-js/ui/src/palette/CommandPalette.tsx:358 — the footer hint row renders literal `esc close` (alongside lines 356 `↑↓ navigate` and 357 `↵ select`), all visible English text not wrapped in t(). This is genuine user-facing text in the palette footer landmark (id={hintId}). NOT a vendored shadcn primitive: the path is packages-js/ui/src/palette/, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*. Ledger scan: Round 1 covered CommandPalette.tsx at lines 274 (sr-only h2), 285 (input placeholder), 291 (live-region noun), and 297 (listbox aria-label) — but the footer hint spans at 356/357/358 are not in any of rounds 1-7. Novel, reproduces, user-facing, not vendored.

confirmed.title: TrashedFilter hardcodes English soft-delete option labels (framework default, never through __())
confirmed.detail: getTypeSpecificProps() serializes three select option labels with hardcoded English fallbacks: 'Without deleted' (line 134), 'With deleted' (line 135), 'Only deleted' (line 136). These are emitted verbatim as the filter dropdown options whenever the app does not call withoutLabel()/withLabel()/onlyLabel(). Framework-hardcoded — unlike SelectFilter (which routes option text through self::localizeOptionLabel()), TrashedFilter does not. The labels render directly in the table filter UI. NOT in ledger (ledger only covers TernaryFilter true/false labels and the JS-side fallback).
confirmed.why_real: Confirmed in source: packages/table/src/Filters/TrashedFilter.php:134-136, getTypeSpecificProps() emits three select option labels with hardcoded English fallbacks ('Without deleted', 'With deleted', 'Only deleted') via $this->withoutLabel ?? 'Without deleted' etc. None route through __() or a localizeOptionLabel() helper. These options serialize as the filter dropdown's visible option text and render verbatim whenever the app does not call withoutLabel()/withLabel()/onlyLabel(). TrashedFilter::make() is the documented framework default, so unmodified apps surface untranslatable English in the table filter UI — a genuine user-facing i18n defect. Contrast with SelectField/SelectFilter which route option labels through localization. Novel: the only packages/table/src/Filters ledger entry is TernaryFilter.php:97 (round 1, a different class covering true/false labels); no TrashedFilter entry exists across rounds 1-7. Not vendored: it is a PHP backend filter class, not under packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*.

confirmed.title: ThemeCommandProvider command-palette labels + category hardcoded English (framework)
confirmed.detail: Three Command DTOs are built with hardcoded user-facing English labels: 'Switch to light theme' (line 35), 'Switch to dark theme' (line 43), 'Use system theme' (line 51), all under category 'Settings' (lines 38/46/54). These Command objects are serialized straight into the CommandPalette UI (a documented, shipped feature). Framework-hardcoded — no __()/trans() anywhere. NOT in ledger (only the JS CommandPalette chrome was covered in round 1).
confirmed.why_real:
Verified against /home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/packages/core/src/CommandPalette/Providers/ThemeCommandProvider.php. The provide() method constructs three Command DTOs with hardcoded English labels: 'Switch to light theme' (line 35), 'Switch to dark theme' (line 43), 'Use system theme' (line 51), each with category 'Settings'. No __()/trans()/Lang anywhere in the file. Command::toArray() (Command.php:54) serializes $this->label verbatim, and these are emitted by a built-in CommandProvider registered into the CommandRegistry, so the strings flow straight into the shipped, documented CommandPalette UI rendered by packages-js/ui/src/palette/CommandPalette.tsx. Genuinely reproduces and is user-facing.

Not a duplicate: I scanned the full R1-R7 ledger. The only CommandPalette entries are round-1 JS-chrome strings in packages-js/ui/src/palette/CommandPalette.tsx (sr-only h2, input placeholder, live-region noun, listbox aria-label). No ledger entry references ThemeCommandProvider, this PHP file path, or the three theme command labels. Verbatim-serialization of PHP-emitted labels is a recurring family in the ledger (Resource labels, Nav labels, Widget headings, Form Section/Tab labels), but these specific Command DTO labels from the theme provider are a distinct, uncovered source.

Not vendored: the file is a framework-authored class under packages/core, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*.

confirmed.title: NavigationCommandProvider builds 'Go to {label}' command + 'Navigation' category from hardcoded English
confirmed.detail: Each resource navigation command is created with label: 'Go to '.$pluralLabel (line 118) and category: 'Navigation' (line 121). The 'Go to ' prefix and the category are hardcoded English concatenated in PHP and serialized verbatim into the command palette. Framework-hardcoded. NOT in ledger.
confirmed.why_real: Confirmed at packages/core/src/CommandPalette/Providers/NavigationCommandProvider.php:116-123: each resource nav Command is built with label: 'Go to '.$pluralLabel (line 118) and category: 'Navigation' (line 121), both hardcoded English concatenated in PHP and serialized verbatim into the command palette payload. User-facing: the command palette renders 'Go to X' entries under a 'Navigation' category heading regardless of locale; neither string passes through __(). Not a vendored shadcn primitive (it is in packages/core PHP). Not in the R1-R7 ledger: ledger CommandPalette entries (round 1) are JS-side UI chrome in CommandPalette.tsx ('Command palette', 'Type a command…', '{n} commands', 'Commands' listbox), and the InertiaDataBuilder.php:363 (round 3) entry covers getPluralLabel serialization in the Inertia nav payload — a different file and code path. No ledger row references NavigationCommandProvider.php, the 'Go to ' prefix, or the 'Navigation' category. Genuinely novel and reproducible.

confirmed.title: Field placeholder & helperText serialized verbatim — localizeLabel() applied to label but NOT to placeholder/helperText
confirmed.detail: getLabel() (line 181) routes the label through self::localizeLabel() so a translation key resolves at serialization time, but getPlaceholder() (line 206-208) and getHelperText() (line 211-213) return the raw stored string with NO localizeLabel() call. FieldSchemaSerializer::serializeOne emits both verbatim ('placeholder' at FieldSchemaSerializer.php:83, 'helperText' at :84). Net effect: an app that sets ->placeholder('arqel::x.y') or a translation key as helper text gets the RAW KEY rendered in the form input, while the sibling label translates correctly. App-must-key, but the framework drops the key on the floor. NOT in ledger (ledger covers Field::$label only).
confirmed.why_real: Verified against source. Field::getLabel() (packages/fields/src/Field.php:181-186) routes the label through self::localizeLabel() (lines 195-204), which calls trans() so a translation key resolves to the active-locale string at serialization time. Field::getPlaceholder() (lines 206-208) and getHelperText() (lines 211-213) return the raw stored string with no localizeLabel() / trans() call. FieldSchemaSerializer::serializeOne emits both verbatim: 'placeholder' => getPlaceholder() at packages/core/src/Support/FieldSchemaSerializer.php:83 and 'helperText' => getHelperText() at :84 — exactly the cited lines. Net effect: an app that sets ->placeholder('arqel::x.y') or a translation key as helper text gets the raw key rendered in the form input while the sibling label correctly translates. This is genuinely user-facing (visible placeholder/helper text in form inputs), reproduces as described, and is framework code (not a vendored packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/* primitive). Ledger check: the only Field.php entry is the round-1 'Field::$label serialized verbatim' (line 181), which is the precursor that ADDED localizeLabel() to the label path only. No ledger entry across R1-R7 covers Field placeholder or helperText; the many other '$label serialized verbatim' entries are for Column/Action/Widget/Resource/nav/form-layout classes, none for Field's placeholder/helperText. Novel, not a duplicate.

confirmed.title: Base table Filter::getLabel() serialized verbatim — never through __()
confirmed.suggested_key: route Filter::getLabel() through a localizeLabel() helper
confirmed.detail: toArray() (line 186-196) emits 'label' => $this->getLabel() (line 191), and getLabel() (line 98-101) returns $this->label ?? $this->name with no translation. This is the filter heading shown above every SelectFilter/TextFilter/DateRangeFilter control in the table filter panel. App-must-key but the key is never resolved. NOT in ledger (ledger covers Column::$label and TernaryFilter sub-labels, not the base Filter label).
confirmed.why_real:
Confirmed in code: Filter::getLabel() (packages/table/src/Filter.php:98-101) returns `$this->label ?? $this->name` with no __() resolution, and toArray() line 191 emits `'label' => $this->getLabel()` verbatim into the Inertia payload. The constructor auto-humanizes the name (line 45) and label() (line 53) sets app-provided labels, but neither path is translated. This label is the user-facing heading rendered above each SelectFilter/TextFilter/DateRangeFilter control in the table filter panel, so it is an app-keyable string that is never resolved — a genuine, reproducible i18n defect.

Not a duplicate: the ledger's nearest table entries are packages/table/src/Column.php:382 (Column::$label — a different class/file) and packages/table/src/Filters/TernaryFilter.php:97 (the trueLabel/falseLabel option sub-labels of a subclass, not the base filter heading). No ledger entry references packages/table/src/Filter.php or the base Filter::getLabel()/toArray() label across rounds 1-7. The serialized-label family in the ledger sweeps Field, Column, Action, Widget, Resource, Nav, Form Section/Tab, Step, Block, KeyValue, Select option labels — but the base Table Filter label is missing from that sweep.

Not vendored: this is a PHP package file, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*.

confirmed.title: QueryBuilder Summary::label serialized verbatim in toArray()
confirmed.detail: final toArray() (line 116) emits 'label' => $this->label (line 121) directly. The Summary label is the user-facing caption of a column footer summary (Count/Sum/Average etc.) rendered in the table footer. Never passed through __(). App-must-key, key never resolves. NOT in ledger.
confirmed.why_real: Verified Summary.php:116-123: final toArray() emits 'label' => $this->label (line 121) verbatim. The label is a user-facing caption (set via constructor or label() setter, lines 31-42) for a column-footer summary (Count/Sum/Average) rendered in the table footer, never passed through __(). App must supply a translation key that never resolves on the JS side. This reproduces and is genuinely user-facing. It belongs to the well-established 'PHP label serialized verbatim, never through __()' family (Column.php:382 R1, NavigationItem/Group R3, Widget R2, Form Tab/Section R7, Block/Step R7), but the ledger has NO entry for packages/table/src/Summaries/Summary.php or any Summaries class — every table-package label entry covers Column.php, a distinct file/class. Not vendored (framework source under packages/table/src, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*). Novel file, real defect.

confirmed.title: QueryBuilder Constraint::toArray serializes label verbatim and raw operator identifiers
confirmed.detail: toArray() (line 101-109) emits 'label' => $this->getLabel() (line 105, getLabel() at line 66 returns Str::headline($this->field) — English humanization — with no __()), and 'operators' => raw operator identifier strings (line 107) such as 'contains'/'equals'/'greater_than' with no localized display labels. When a QueryBuilderFilter UI consumes these, both the constraint label and operator names render English-only. Framework-hardcoded humanization + app-must-key label. NOT in ledger.
confirmed.why_real:
Verified at packages/table/src/Filters/Constraints/Constraint.php: toArray() (lines 101-109) emits 'label' => $this->getLabel() (line 105). getLabel() (lines 66-69) returns $this->label ?? Str::headline($this->field) — English humanization with no __() wrapping. Line 107 emits 'operators' => $this->getOperators() as raw identifier strings ('contains'/'equals'/etc.) with no localized display labels. Confirmed this payload reaches the frontend: QueryBuilderFilter.php lines 171-173 map every constraint through $c->toArray() into the filter's serialized props, so both the constraint label and operator names render English-only in the Visual Query Builder UI.

NOT a duplicate: scanned the full ledger (rounds 1-7). Many packages/table/* entries exist (Column.php:382, TernaryFilter.php:97, DateColumn.php:53) but NONE reference packages/table/src/Filters/Constraints/Constraint.php. This file is absent from all 7 rounds.

NOT vendored: it is a PHP file in packages/table, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*.

Genuinely user-facing and matches the exact defect class the ledger has repeatedly accepted ('label serialized verbatim / English humanization fallback, never through __()' — e.g. Column.php:382, NavigationItem.php:212, Widget.php:238). The label half is a clean reproduction of that accepted pattern. Confidence is med rather than high only because the operator-identifier half is arguably a frontend-mapping concern rather than a strict PHP __() omission, but the label half alone is a solid, novel, user-facing i18n defect.

confirmed.title: ActionController bulk invoke aborts with hardcoded 'Missing selection.'
confirmed.detail: invokeBulk() calls abort(422, 'Missing selection.') (line 90) when no rows are selected. This 422 surfaces to the user via Inertia error flash when a bulk action is triggered without a selection. Hardcoded English, no trans(). Framework-hardcoded. NOT in ledger (round 6 covered ActionController validation-attribute gap at line 115, not this abort).
confirmed.why_real: Confirmed by reading packages/actions/src/Http/Controllers/ActionController.php:90: invokeBulk() calls abort(HttpResponse::HTTP_UNPROCESSABLE_ENTITY, 'Missing selection.') — a hardcoded English string passed verbatim, never routed through trans()/__(). It is framework code (packages/actions/), not a vendored shadcn primitive. NOT a duplicate: the only ActionController ledger entry is round 6 / line 115 (form-validation :attribute / custom messages/attributes gap), a distinct concern; this line-90 abort message is novel. User-facing: a 422 abort surfaces to the user via Inertia's error flash. Reachability is marginal under normal use because the frontend only renders bulk-action buttons when selectedIds.length > 0 (ArqelIndexPage.tsx:254) and clears nothing otherwise, so this is primarily a defensive server-side guard hit via forged/direct requests or a selection-cleared race. However, the project's accepted bar already includes equally low-reach hardcoded abort messages (ExportDownloadController abort messages round 2, LocaleController 'Invalid locale.' round 2, audit 403/400 messages round 6), so by the established ledger standard this is a real i18n defect. Confidence med due to the marginal user-facing reach.

confirmed.title: RequireTenantFeature aborts with hardcoded 'No current tenant.'
confirmed.detail: abort(404, 'No current tenant.') (line 46) when no tenant is resolved. Hardcoded English. The sibling feature-unavailable message at line 60 was already localized in round 5 (arqel::messages.tenant.feature_unavailable), but this earlier no-tenant abort was missed. User-facing 404 message. Framework-hardcoded. NOT in ledger. (The trait-missing abort at line 50-53 is a developer config error — excluded.)
confirmed.why_real: Confirmed at packages/tenant/src/Middleware/RequireTenantFeature.php:46: abort(Response::HTTP_NOT_FOUND, 'No current tenant.') passes a hardcoded English string as the HTTP 404 abort message. This is user-facing (surfaces as the response status message / error payload) and framework-hardcoded, unlike the sibling line 60 feature_unavailable message which uses __('arqel::messages.tenant.feature_unavailable'). Ledger scan: the only entry for this file is round 5 at line 60 (plan-restriction message) — line 46 is a distinct location, NOT in the R1-R7 ledger. Not a vendored shadcn primitive (it lives in packages/tenant PHP middleware, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*). The line 49-53 trait-missing abort is correctly excluded as a developer-config error, not user-facing. Genuine, novel, user-facing i18n defect.

confirmed.title: FieldUploadController user-facing 4xx upload errors hardcoded English
confirmed.detail: Multiple abort() messages that can surface when a file-upload field posts: 'Missing uploaded file.' (line 66), 'Could not persist uploaded file.' (line 78), 'Missing file path.' (line 113), 'Invalid file path.' (line 170), 'File path is outside the allowed directory.' (lines 175/186/198), 'Field is not a file upload.' (lines 55/105). All hardcoded English, no trans(). Currently the JS FileInput does not surface the server message body, so these are mostly devtools/API-facing today, but they are user-routable abort strings on the file-upload path. Framework-hardcoded. NOT in ledger.
confirmed.why_real: Confirmed by reading packages/fields/src/Http/Controllers/FieldUploadController.php. All cited abort() messages exist verbatim with hardcoded English and no trans()/__(): line 55 & 105 'Field is not a file upload.', line 66 'Missing uploaded file.', line 78 'Could not persist uploaded file.', line 113 'Missing file path.', line 170 'Invalid file path.', lines 175/186/198 'File path is outside the allowed directory.'. These are passed directly as the abort() message on a real user-routable HTTP endpoint (POST/DELETE {panel}/{resource}/fields/{field}/upload). Not in the R1-R7 ledger: grepped all ~130 entries; the only related abort-message entry is round 2 ExportDownloadController.php:41 (different file/package), plus round 5/6 audit + LocaleController entries — none reference FieldUploadController. Not a vendored shadcn primitive (it's a PHP controller, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*). The finding honestly notes the JS FileInput does not currently surface the server body, but the ledger has repeatedly accepted server abort-message i18n defects on identical grounds (export/audit/locale controllers), so this is consistent precedent. Genuine, novel, framework-hardcoded i18n defect.

confirmed.title: Pulse AI-tokens card: number_format() emits en-US grouping
confirmed.detail: `{{ number_format($tokens) }}` uses PHP's default number_format() which hardcodes ',' thousands / '.' decimal regardless of app locale. A pt_BR viewer sees '1,234,567' instead of '1.234.567'. This Pulse dashboard card is rendered in the admin panel and is user-facing. Repro: set app()->setLocale('pt_BR'), record >999 AI tokens, open the Arqel AI Tokens Pulse card — grouping is comma-separated (en-US) not locale-aware. Fix: format via Laravel's Number::format($tokens) (locale-aware) or pass an explicit locale.
confirmed.why_real:
Verified at packages/core/resources/views/pulse/ai-tokens.blade.php:12: `{{ number_format($tokens) }}`. PHP's single-argument number_format() hardcodes ',' thousands / '.' decimal and never derives from app()->getLocale(), so a pt_BR viewer sees '1,234,567' instead of '1.234.567' — locale-insensitive grouping, accurately described.

User-facing: yes. The view is rendered by Arqel\Core\Pulse\Cards\ArqelAiTokensCard (packages/core/src/Pulse/Cards/ArqelAiTokensCard.php:44) and registered via PulseIntegration.php:46 ('arqel-ai-tokens-card'), shown on the Laravel Pulse /pulse admin dashboard. Caveat: only active when laravel/pulse is installed (optional integration, no-op otherwise) — this narrows the surface but does not make it not-a-bug.

Not vendored: it is an Arqel-authored Blade view in packages/core, not a packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/* primitive.

Not in ledger: full scan of /home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/reports/i18n-fixed-ledger.json returns 0 references to 'pulse' or 'ai-tokens.blade.php' across all R1-R7 entries. The number-grouping defect class itself IS recognized as valid in the ledger (R2 'NumberCell defaults to en-US grouping', R7 'RichTextInput counter emits un-grouped numbers'), and the same Pulse Blade card was never covered, so this is a novel instance. The named line 12 is correct (line 18 $cost shares the bug but is out of scope of this finding).

Confidence med rather than high only because the surface is behind an optional dependency, but the defect genuinely reproduces, is novel, user-facing, and Arqel-owned.

confirmed.title: Pulse AI-tokens card: hardcoded '$' USD currency symbol + en-US number_format on cost
confirmed.detail: `${{ number_format($cost, 4) }}` hardcodes the '$' (USD) currency symbol as a literal prefix and formats the amount with en-US grouping/decimal. The label says 'Cost (USD)' but the symbol position and decimal mark are locale-insensitive: a pt_BR viewer expects 'US$ 0,0012' / 'R$' positioning, not '$0.0012'. Repro: locale=pt_BR, view AI Tokens card — cost renders '$0.0012' (en-US) not a locale-formatted currency. Fix: Number::currency($cost, 'USD') which yields the correctly-localized symbol, placement and decimal mark.
confirmed.why_real: Verified at packages/core/resources/views/pulse/ai-tokens.blade.php:18 — the line is literally `${{ number_format($cost, 4) }}`. This hardcodes a `$` literal currency prefix and formats the amount with PHP number_format, which uses en-US grouping/decimal marks (',' thousands, '.' decimal) regardless of app locale. A pt_BR operator sees `$0.0012` rather than a localized `US$ 0,0012`. Not vendored: it's a project-authored Blade view in packages/core, not under packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*. Not in the R1-R7 ledger: scanned all entries; no Pulse view or ai-tokens.blade.php appears, and the existing currency/number entries (CurrencyField.php, CurrencyInput.tsx, NumberCell, AiImageInput file-size, export date cells) are different files. It is the same defect family as the confirmed NumberCell en-US grouping (R2) but a distinct, novel location. User-facing on the Pulse monitoring dashboard. Severity is lower (it is an internal ops card and the value genuinely is USD, so the '$' symbol partly matches the 'Cost (USD)' label), but the decimal/grouping marks are genuinely locale-insensitive and reproduce, so it qualifies as a real i18n defect. The suggested Number::currency($cost,'USD') fix is appropriate.

confirmed.title: Pulse Resources card: number_format() en-US grouping
confirmed.detail: `{{ number_format($count) }}` hardcodes en-US thousands/decimal separators. With >999 registered resources a pt_BR/de_DE viewer sees the wrong grouping mark. Repro: locale=pt_BR → '1,234' instead of '1.234'. Fix: Number::format($count).
confirmed.why_real: Verified packages/core/resources/views/pulse/resources.blade.php:15 contains `{{ number_format($count) }}`, which hardcodes en-US grouping (',' thousands / '.' decimal). PHP's number_format() with a single argument ignores the application locale, so a pt_BR/de_DE viewer with >999 registered resources sees '1,234' instead of the locale-correct '1.234'. The fix (Number::format($count), Laravel's locale-aware helper) is appropriate. The card is genuinely wired: ArqelResourcesCard (packages/core/src/Pulse/Cards/ArqelResourcesCard.php) renders this view via PulseIntegration, and it's exercised by PulseIntegrationTest, so this is live user-facing admin UI, not dead code. Not a vendored shadcn primitive (it's a Laravel Pulse Blade card under packages/core, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*). Not a duplicate: the ledger has no entry for any pulse/*.blade.php file; the related number-format entries are NumberCell in cells.tsx:169 (round 2) and the RichTextInput counter (round 7) — different files, different layers (React vs Blade), different surfaces. Confidence is high on the technical defect and novelty; the only caveat is severity (the wrong grouping only manifests at a count >999, which is unrealistic for Resource class counts), but the rule asks for a real, reproducible, novel, user-facing i18n defect, which this satisfies.

confirmed.title: Pulse Resources card: naive English pluralization "Resource(s)"
confirmed.detail: `Resource{{ $count === 1 ? '' : 's' }} registered` hardcodes English singular/plural morphology and the English word 'Resource ... registered' inline, with no trans()/lang key. Non-English panels show English, and the s-suffix pluralization rule is English-only (breaks for locales with >2 plural categories). Repro: locale=pt_BR → still renders 'Resources registered'. Fix: a trans_choice() key with proper {one}/{other} CLDR forms, e.g. arqel::pulse.resources_registered.
confirmed.why_real: Verified at packages/core/resources/views/pulse/resources.blade.php:18: `Resource{{ $count === 1 ? '' : 's' }} registered` hardcodes the English word and an English-only s-suffix pluralization rule with no trans()/trans_choice() key. Reproduces as described: a pt_BR panel renders English, and the ternary plural rule breaks for locales with >2 CLDR plural categories. Not vendored (it is a framework-authored Pulse blade card under packages/core, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*). Not in the R1-R7 ledger: no entry references any pulse blade view; the nearest pluralization entries (TableToolbar.tsx:43 round 3, useTranslator round 6) are different files in the JS layer. Novel and user-facing (Pulse cards are visible admin UI). Confidence is med rather than high only because this is a Laravel Pulse ops-dashboard integration whose adjacent header strings (lines 3-4) are also English by design, so whether this ops surface is within the i18n sweep's intended end-user scope is arguable; the defect itself reproduces exactly.

confirmed.title: Pulse Job-metrics card: number_format() en-US grouping (pending/failed)
confirmed.detail: `{{ number_format($pending) }}` (line 12) and `{{ number_format($failed) }}` (line 18) both use en-US default grouping/decimal separators regardless of app locale. Repro: locale=pt_BR with >999 pending jobs → '1,234' not '1.234'. Fix: Number::format(...).
confirmed.why_real: Confirmed at packages/core/resources/views/pulse/job-metrics.blade.php: line 12 `{{ number_format($pending) }}` and line 18 `{{ number_format($failed) }}` both call PHP's number_format() with no locale argument, which always emits en-US grouping (',' thousands / '.' decimal). Under app locale pt_BR a count >999 (e.g. 1234) renders '1,234' instead of the locale-correct '1.234' — a genuine user-facing locale-format defect on a Pulse dashboard card. Fix is Laravel's locale-aware Number::format(). NOT a duplicate: the R1-R7 ledger contains no entry for this file or for Pulse job-metrics; the only number-grouping entries (NumberCell round 2, RichTextInput counter round 7) are different files in the JS layer. NOT a vendored shadcn primitive — it lives under packages/core/resources/views/, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*. Novel, reproducible, user-facing.

confirmed.title: Pulse Top-actions card: number_format() en-US grouping on count column
confirmed.detail: `{{ number_format($row['count']) }}` in the action-count table cell hardcodes en-US separators. A high-volume action's count renders with comma grouping for all locales. Repro: locale=de_DE, action with >999 executions → '1,234' instead of '1.234'. Fix: Number::format($row['count']).
confirmed.why_real: Verified line 24 of packages/core/resources/views/pulse/top-actions.blade.php: `{{ number_format($row['count']) }}`. Single-arg number_format() uses PHP en-US defaults (',' thousands, '.' decimal), so a count >999 renders '1,234' for all locales including de_DE where '1.234' is expected — the defect reproduces as described. Not vendored: the file is a Pulse card blade in packages/core, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*. Not in the R1-R7 ledger: no entry references this file or any Pulse/blade number_format; existing number-format entries are all distinct JS contexts (cells.tsx NumberCell, RichTextInput counter, AiImageInput file-size, KeyValueField etc.). Novel, genuinely reproduces, and renders a user-visible number in a shipped view. Minor caveat: the whole Pulse card is hardcoded-English and outside Arqel's i18n dictionary pipeline, so it is an operator-facing observability panel rather than an end-user localized surface — but the locale-format claim itself is accurate.
